steps.title: Identify the key phrase marking Spanish's role
steps.content: The strongest signal is “entered English through Spanish.” The word “through” is key—it marks Spanish as an intermediary conduit between Indigenous languages and English.

steps.title: Match the transmission role to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C identifies Spanish as a “medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English”—the exact relationship signaled by “through.” The other choices claim bidirectional exchange with Indigenous languages, compare contribution volumes the passage doesn't address, or treat two examples as proof of equal adoption rates.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
